subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Reference (Step 0 Audit)
- **Standard game** — 2 to 8 players.
- **Recommended** — 4 to 5 players for best balance of interaction and game length.

#### Board
- **Game board** — 1 folding board with a square track of 40 spaces.

#### Money
- **Monopoly money** — Bills in denominations: $1, $5, $10, $20, $50, $100, $500.
- **Total bank supply** — The bank starts with a large supply of each denomination (varies by edition, bank never runs out — make extra bills from paper if needed).

#### Property Cards
- **Title deed cards** — 28 property cards: 22 color-group streets, 4 railroads, 2 utilities.

#### Buildings
- **Houses** — 32 green plastic houses.
- **Hotels** — 12 red plastic hotels.

#### Cards
- **Community Chest** — 16 cards.
- **Chance** — 16 cards.

#### Other Components
- **Dice** — 2 standard six-sided dice.
- **Player tokens** — 8 metal tokens (varies by edition: car, top hat, dog, iron, battleship, etc.).
- **Rulebook** — 1 rulebook.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Board Track (40 Spaces)
- **GO (corner)** — Starting/passing corner. Collect $200 when passing or landing on GO.
- **Properties** — 22 colored street properties in 8 color groups (2-3 per group).
- **Railroads** — 4 railroad spaces (Reading, Pennsylvania, B&O, Short Line).
- **Utilities** — 2 utility spaces (Electric Company, Water Works).
- **Community Chest** — 3 spaces triggering a Community Chest card draw.
- **Chance** — 3 spaces triggering a Chance card draw.
- **Tax spaces** — Income Tax ($200) and Luxury Tax ($100).
- **Jail / Just Visiting (corner)** — Landing on "Just Visiting" has no penalty. Sent to Jail is different.
- **Free Parking (corner)** — No official effect (common house rule: collect accumulated money).
- **Go to Jail (corner)** — Landing here sends you directly to Jail without passing GO.

#### Color Groups
- **Brown (dark purple)** — Mediterranean Ave, Baltic Ave (2 properties).
- **Light Blue** — Oriental Ave, Vermont Ave, Connecticut Ave (3 properties).
- **Pink (magenta)** — St. Charles Place, States Ave, Virginia Ave (3 properties).
- **Orange** — St. James Place, Tennessee Ave, New York Ave (3 properties).
- **Red** — Kentucky Ave, Indiana Ave, Illinois Ave (3 properties).
- **Yellow** — Atlantic Ave, Ventnor Ave, Marvin Gardens (3 properties).
- **Green** — Pacific Ave, North Carolina Ave, Pennsylvania Ave (3 properties).
- **Dark Blue** — Park Place, Boardwalk (2 properties).

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Starting Money
- **Each player receives $1,500** distributed as:
  - 2 x $500
  - 2 x $100
  - 2 x $50
  - 6 x $20
  - 5 x $10
  - 5 x $5
  - 5 x $1

#### Banker
- **Select a banker** — One player manages the bank's money, property cards, houses, and hotels.
- **Bank is separate** — The banker must keep bank money strictly separate from their personal funds.

#### Token Placement
- **Choose a token** — Each player selects a metal playing piece.
- **Place on GO** — All tokens start on the GO corner.

#### Determining First Player
- **Roll dice** — Each player rolls both dice. Highest total goes first.
- **Turn order** — Play proceeds clockwise.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Pre-Game Setup
1. **Shuffle cards** — Shuffle Community Chest and Chance decks separately. Place face-down on their designated board spaces.
2. **Organize bank** — Sort all remaining money, property title deeds, houses, and hotels near the board.
3. **All properties unowned** — Title deed cards start in the bank, not dealt to players.

#### First Turn
1. **Roll both dice** — The first player rolls.
2. **Move** — Advance your token clockwise that many spaces.
3. **Resolve space** — Follow the rules for the space you land on.
4. **Doubles rule** — If you roll doubles, take your turn, then roll again. Three consecutive doubles = go directly to Jail.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Turn Sequence
1. **Roll both dice** — Roll 2 six-sided dice.
2. **Move** — Advance your token clockwise the total number of spaces shown.
3. **Pass GO** — If you pass or land on GO, collect $200 from the bank.
4. **Resolve the space** — Take action based on where you land (see Actions & Options).
5. **Doubles** — If you rolled doubles, take another full turn after resolving. Three doubles in a row = go directly to Jail.
6. **End turn** — If no doubles, play passes clockwise.

#### Between Turns
- **Trading** — Players may trade properties, cash, and Get Out of Jail Free cards with each other at any time, even between turns.
- **Building** — You may buy houses/hotels between turns (on your own color groups) during your turn or between other players' turns.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Landing on Unowned Property
- **Buy it** — Pay the listed price to the bank and receive the title deed card.
- **Decline to buy** — If you choose not to buy, the bank immediately auctions the property to all players (including you). Bidding starts at any amount. Highest bidder pays the bank and receives the deed.

#### Landing on Owned Property
- **Pay rent** — Pay the owner the rent shown on the title deed.
- **Base rent** — Printed on the card for an undeveloped property.
- **Color group bonus** — If the owner holds all properties in a color group (with no mortgages), base rent on undeveloped properties is doubled.
- **Houses** — Rent increases significantly with each house (1-4 houses).
- **Hotel** — Maximum development. Highest rent tier.
- **Railroad rent** — $25 for 1 railroad, $50 for 2, $100 for 3, $200 for all 4.
- **Utility rent** — 1 utility: 4x dice roll. Both utilities: 10x dice roll.

#### Landing on Special Spaces
- **Community Chest** — Draw top card, follow instructions, return card to bottom of deck.
- **Chance** — Draw top card, follow instructions, return card to bottom of deck.
- **Income Tax** — Pay $200 to the bank.
- **Luxury Tax** — Pay $100 to the bank.
- **Go to Jail** — Move directly to Jail. Do not pass GO. Do not collect $200.
- **Free Parking** — Nothing happens (official rules). No money is collected.
- **Just Visiting** — If you land on the Jail space normally, you are "Just Visiting" with no penalty.

#### Mortgage
- **Mortgage a property** — Flip the title deed to its mortgage side and collect the mortgage value from the bank.
- **No rent** — Mortgaged properties cannot collect rent.
- **Unmortgage** — Pay the mortgage value plus 10% interest to the bank and flip the deed back.
- **Cannot build** — You cannot build on any property in a color group if any property in that group is mortgaged.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Building Houses and Hotels
- **Complete color group required** — You must own all properties in a color group (none mortgaged) before building.
- **Even building rule** — Houses must be built evenly across the group. You cannot place a second house on any property until all properties in the group have one house.
- **Hotel upgrade** — After 4 houses on each property in the group, you may upgrade to a hotel (return 4 houses + pay hotel price).
- **Building shortage** — If the bank runs out of houses, no more houses can be built. This is intentional. Players may not substitute.
- **Selling buildings** — Sell houses/hotels back to the bank at half purchase price. Hotels must be downgraded evenly.

#### Jail
- **How you go to Jail** — Land on "Go to Jail" space, draw a "Go to Jail" card, or roll 3 consecutive doubles.
- **In Jail** — You cannot move but may still collect rent, trade, and build.
- **Getting out of Jail** (choose one at the start of your turn):
  - Pay $50 before rolling.
  - Use a Get Out of Jail Free card.
  - Roll doubles on your turn (you move but do not get an extra turn for doubles).
- **3-turn limit** — If you have not rolled doubles after 3 turns in Jail, you must pay $50 and move the amount shown on your third roll.

#### Trading
- **What can be traded** — Properties (unmortgaged or mortgaged), cash, and Get Out of Jail Free cards.
- **No building trades** — Houses and hotels cannot be traded; they must be sold back to the bank.
- **No loans** — Players cannot loan money to each other. Deals must be completed immediately.
- **Mortgaged property trades** — When receiving a mortgaged property, you may pay off the mortgage immediately or pay 10% interest now and the full mortgage + 10% later to unmortgage.

#### Bankruptcy
- **Owed to the bank** — If you cannot pay the bank, sell buildings at half price and mortgage properties. If still unable to pay, you are bankrupt. All your assets go to the bank; properties are auctioned.
- **Owed to a player** — If you cannot pay another player, all your assets transfer to that player (who must immediately pay 10% interest on any mortgaged properties received, or unmortgage them).

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Standard Endgame
- **Last player standing** — The game ends when all but one player have gone bankrupt. The remaining player wins.

#### Speed/Timed Rules (Official Variant)
- **Time limit** — Set a timer before starting (e.g., 60 or 90 minutes).
- **Richest player wins** — When time expires, each player totals their assets:
  - Cash on hand.
  - Properties at printed purchase price (mortgaged properties at half).
  - Houses at purchase price.
  - Hotels at purchase price plus 4 houses' worth.
- **Highest total wins.**

#### Scoring Model
- **Elimination format** — Standard Monopoly has no point system. Players are eliminated by bankruptcy.
- **Asset counting** — Only used in timed/speed variants.

#### Tiebreaker (Timed Game)
- **Cash tiebreaker** — If total assets are tied, the player with more cash on hand wins.
- **If still tied** — The player with more properties wins.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Property Acquisition
- **Buy everything early** — In the early game, buy every property you land on. Having properties gives trading leverage and prevents opponents from completing color groups.
- **Auctions are opportunities** — If someone declines a property, bid aggressively on properties that complete or block color groups.
- **Target the orange group** — Statistically, St. James Place, Tennessee Ave, and New York Ave are landed on most frequently (due to Jail placement and dice probabilities).

#### Cash Management
- **Keep a reserve** — Maintain at least $200-300 for rent payments. Going broke early from overbidding is a common trap.
- **Mortgaging is fine** — Do not be afraid to mortgage low-value properties to fund house building on a complete color group.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
#### Frequent Errors
- **Not buying properties** — Passing on early purchases to "save money" is almost always wrong. Properties generate income; cash sitting idle does not.
- **Ignoring auctions** — When a player declines to buy, the auction often sells the property below face value. Always participate.
- **Uneven trading** — Trading away a property that completes an opponent's color group without getting equivalent value (or completing your own group) is a losing move.
- **Building too slowly** — Once you have a color group, build to 3 houses per property as quickly as possible. The rent jump from 2 to 3 houses is massive.
- **Staying in Jail too early** — In the early game, you want to move around the board and buy properties. Pay $50 to get out quickly. In the late game, Jail is safe (you collect rent while avoiding opponents' hotels).
- **House rules that extend the game** — Free Parking money, no auctions, and loans between players all make the game significantly longer than intended.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: When to Trade?
- **Trade to complete** — A fair trade that gives both players a complete color group is usually worth it. The player who builds first has the advantage.
- **Block trades** — Sometimes holding a single property to prevent an opponent from completing their group is more valuable than any trade offer.

#### Decision 2: Where to Build?
- **3 houses is the sweet spot** — Rent increases most dramatically from 2 to 3 houses. Prioritize getting all properties in a group to 3 houses before going to 4 or hotels.
- **Housing shortage strategy** — With only 32 houses in the game, building 4 houses on each property (without upgrading to hotels) can lock out other players from building.

#### Decision 3: Jail Strategy
- **Early game — get out fast** — You need to be moving and buying. Pay $50 or use a card immediately.
- **Late game — stay in** — When the board is full of opponents' hotels, Jail is the safest place. Collect rent while opponents land on your properties. Try to roll doubles; if not, pay after 3 turns.

#### Decision 4: Mortgage or Sell Buildings?
- **Mortgage undeveloped properties first** — Mortgaging costs only 10% interest. Selling buildings at half price is a steeper loss.
- **Keep income properties active** — Never mortgage a property in a developed color group (you cannot collect rent on any of them if one is mortgaged).
